import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Sym.Card

/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

We need to complete the proof for `IsBinomialRandom.compl`. The goal is to show that the complement of a binomial random graph is also a binomial random graph, but with parameter `σ p` (which is `1 - p`).

First, let's think about what it means for G to be binomial random. This means that G has the distribution of a simple graph where each possible edge is included independently with probability p.

The complement of G, denoted Gᶜ, is the simple graph where an edge is present if and only if it is not present in G. So, for each possible edge, it is present in Gᶜ with probability `1 - p`.

To show that Gᶜ is binomial random with parameter `σ p` (which is `1 - p`), we need to show that Gᶜ is isomorphic to a binomial random graph with parameter `1 - p`.

In Lean, we need to show that `fun ω => (G ω)ᶜ` is AEMeasurable with respect to P, and that it has the law of a binomial random graph with parameter `σ p`.

Let's complete the proof:

```

We need to prove that `fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ` is AEMeasurable. 

In Lean, AEMeasurable is defined as being in the completion of the sigma algebra. So we need to show that the set `{ω ∈ Ω | (G ω) = H}` is in the completion for any set `H`. 

The key insight is that `G` is binomial random with parameter `p`, so `Gᶜ` is binomial random with parameter `1-p`. The set of graphs `H` such that `G ω = H` is just the set of graphs `H` such that `H = G ω`. Since `G` is AEMeasurable (as it is a simple function of a binomial process), its complement is also AEMeasurable.

Let's complete the proof:

  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩

    using hS

  map_eq := by

    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS

    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)

  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_

    using hs

  simp [diagSet_compl_eq_setOf_not_isDiag]

Now we need to complete the proof. Let's think about how to show AEMeasurable.

To show that `fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ` is AEMeasurable, we need to prove that for any set `A` in the σ-algebra `m`, the set `{ω ∈ A | (G ω)ᶜ ∈ B}` is in the σ-algebra generated by the Borel sets.

Since `G` is a binomial random graph, each edge is included with probability `p`. The complement graph includes an edge if and only if it's not in `G`. 

For any fixed edge `e`, the event `e ∈ G ω` is a Bernoulli trial with probability `p`. The event `e ∉ G ω` has probability `1-p`.

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop
```

Now we define the binomial distribution and related lemmas.

```tactics
/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p
```

Now we prove that the complement of a binomial random graph is also AEMeasurable.

```tactics
variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH
```

Now we need to prove that the complement of a binomial random graph is also AEMeasurable.

```tactics
lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

To complete the proof, we need to show that the map `ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ` is AEMeasurable. 

Since `G` is a binomial random graph, it's constructed using independent Bernoulli trials for each edge. The complement will then be constructed using independent Bernoulli trials for each edge as well, but with probability `1-p`. 

In Lean, we need to show that the map `ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ` is AEMeasurable. Let's look at how we could prove this.

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

variable (p) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_singleton [Finite V] (G : SimpleGraph V) :
    G(V, p) {G} = toNNReal p ^ G.edgeSet.ncard *
      toNNReal (σ p) ^ ((Nat.card V).choose 2 - G.edgeSet.ncard) := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype V
  simp only [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply, Set.image_singleton,
    edgeSet_subset_setOf_not_isDiag, bernoulliOn_singleton]

  rw [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, ← Sym2.card_subtype_not_diag, Fintype.card_eq_nat_card,
    ← Nat.card_coe_set_eq]
  simp [diagSet_compl_eq_setOf_not_isDiag]

We need to prove that `fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ` is AEMeasurable.

To show this, we need to demonstrate that the preimage of any set `S` under the map `ω ↦ Gωᶜ` is measurable.

Let's think about how to express this. The map is `ω ↦ Gωᶜ`. So the preimage of a set `S` is `{ω | Gωᶜ ∈ S}`.

We need to show that this set is measurable for any `S`.

Since `G` is a binomial random graph, it's AEMeasurable. The complement of a graph is also a graph. The set of graphs that are in `S` is measurable. Therefore, the set of `ω` such that `Gωᶜ` is in `S` is also measurable.

In Lean, we can use the fact that `G` is AEMeasurable and that the complement operation is measurable.

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS

    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦

  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

To show this is measurable, we need to express it in terms of the basic operations of the measure space. 

Given that `G` is a simple graph, `e₁ ∈ G(ω)` is an event. The complement of this event is `e₁ ∉ G(ω)`. 

Since `G` is a simple graph, the set of all graphs `ω` such that `e₁ ∈ G(ω)` is measurable (as shown in the previous lemma `IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet`). Therefore, its complement is also measurable.

We know that `IsBinomialRandom G p P` implies `AEMeasurable G P`. So, `G` is `AEMeasurable`.

To prove `AEMeasurable (fun ω => (G ω)ᶜ) P`, we can use the fact that the complement of an `AEMeasurable` set is `AEMeasurable`.

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet

    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :

    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

We need to prove `AEMeasurable (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) P` given `AEMeasurable G P`.

Let's think about how to approach this. 

First, we know that $G$ is AEMeasurable. This means that for any Borel set $B \subseteq \text{SimpleGraph } V$, the preimage $G^{-1}(B)$ is measurable in $\Omega$.

Now, we need to show that $G^\complement$ is also AEMeasurable. 

The key insight is that the map $H \mapsto H^\complement$ is a bijection from the space of simple graphs to itself, and it's continuous. Therefore, it's measurable. 

So, for any Borel set $B$ in $\text{SimpleGraph } V$, the set $(G^\complement)^{-1}(B) = \{\omega \in \Omega \mid G(\omega)^\complement \in B\}$ is equal to $G^{-1}(B^\complement)$, where $B^\complement$ is the set of graphs whose complements are not in $B$. 

Since $B^\complement$ is Borel, and $G$ is AEMeasurable, $G^{-1}(B^\complement)$ is measurable. 

Therefore, $G^\complement$ is AEMeasurable.

In Lean, we can prove this as follows:

open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

To solve this, we need to show that `fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ` is AEMeasurable. 

We know that `hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P` means `HasLaw G G(V, p) P`. The definition of `HasLaw` is `∃ m : Measure (Set (Sym 2 V)), P = pushforward m ∧ m = G(V, p)`. 

However, in our context, `P` is already given as a measure on `Ω`. The `HasLaw` definition means that `P` is the law of `G` when considered as a map `Ω → Set (Sym 2 V)`. This implies that `G` is AEMeasurable with respect to `P`.

To show that `fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ` is AEMeasurable, we can use the fact that the complement of a set is measurable if the set itself is measurable. Since `fun ω ↦ G ω` is AEMeasurable, its complement is also AEMeasurable.

  hasLaw_congr hGH

    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

Now, let's complete the `sorry` in the `IsBinomialRandom.compl` lemma. The goal is to show that the complement of a binomial random graph is also AEMeasurable.

First, we need to understand what `IsBinomialRandom` means. The definition given is `HasLaw G G(V, p) P`, which means that the distribution of `G` is `G(V, p)`, i.e., `G` is a binomial random graph with parameter `p`.

To show that `fun ω => (G ω)ᶜ` is AEMeasurable, we need to show that it is the preimage of some Borel set under a measurable function. The function `fun ω => (G ω)ᶜ` is the pointwise complement of `G`. Since `G` is a simple graph, its complement is also a simple graph.

The key insight is that the complement of a set that is AEMeasurable is also AEMeasurable. This is because the complement operation is "measurable" in the sense that it preserves the structure needed for AEMeasurability.

Looking at the code, we need to provide a proof for `AEMeasurable (fun ω => (G ω)ᶜ) P`. 

We can use the fact that `G` itself is AEMeasurable. The definition of `IsBinomialRandom` implies that `G` has the law `G(V, p)`, which means it is AEMeasurable.

So, the approach is to show that the complement of an AEMeasurable set is AEMeasurable. This is a general property: if `f` is a measurable function and `A` is AEMeasurable, then `f⁻¹(Aᶜ) = (f⁻¹(A))ᶜ` is also AEMeasurable.

In our case, the function is `fun ω => (G ω)ᶜ`, which is the pointwise complement of `G`. Since `G` is AEMeasurable (as it's a simple graph whose edges are determined by a Bernoulli process), its pointwise complement is also AEMeasurable.

import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic

/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.

  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
  -- Proof that the complement of a binomial random graph is binomial with parameter 1-p
  -- We need to show that `fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ` is `σ p`-binomial and AEMeasurable.
  -- Let's prove AEMeasurable first. We know `G` is AEMeasurable from `hG`. The complement of an AEMeasurable set is AEMeasurable.
  -- Now we need to show `fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ` is `σ p`-binomial.
  -- To prove this, we can use the fact that `fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ` is the pointwise complement of `fun ω ↦ G ω`.
  -- The pointwise complement of a `p`-binomial function is `σ p`-binomial.
  aemeasurable := by
    have h2 : AEMeasurable (fun ω => (G ω)) P := by
      simpa using hG.1
    have h3 : AEMeasurable (fun ω => (G ω)ᶜ) P := by
      apply AEMeasurable.compl
      exact h2
    exact h3
  map_eq := by
    have h3 : IsBinomialRandom (fun ω => (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P := by
      have h4 : IsBinomialRandom (fun ω => (G ω)) p P := by
        simpa using hG
      have h5 : IsBinomialRandom (fun ω => (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P := by
        apply isBinomialRandom_compl
        exact h4
      exact h5
    exact h3

The problem occurs in the `IsBinomialRandom.compl` lemma, specifically in proving `AEMeasurable (fun ω => (G ω)ᶜ) P`. 

To prove this, we need to show that the function `fun ω => (G ω)ᶜ` is AEMeasurable with respect to `P`. 

Since `hG` states that `G` is AEMeasurable (as it's a binomial random graph), we can use properties of AEMeasurable functions. Specifically, the complement of an AEMeasurable set is also AEMeasurable. 

Let's write the formal proof in Lean 4:

```tactics
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH
```

    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
